rendering_standard: SVG inline, viewBox 700x550 for complex graphs, aria-label required. Answer NEVER encoded in visual. Asymptotes drawn as dashed lines always.

visual_generators.description: Coordinate plane with a function graphed (with possible endpoints, gaps, or asymptotes); Mathlete identifies domain, range, intervals of increase/decrease, or end behavior
visual_generators.format_hint_rule: Interval labels NOT annotated — Mathlete reads from graph

visual_generators.description: Coordinate plane showing f(x) and f⁻¹(x) reflected over y = x; dashed y = x line shown; Mathlete identifies which is f and which is f⁻¹
visual_generators.format_hint_rule: Inverse is shown but NOT labeled — Mathlete identifies by symmetry over y = x

visual_generators.description: Coordinate plane with a piecewise function graphed; open/closed circles at boundaries; Mathlete writes the piecewise definition OR evaluates at a specific input
visual_generators.format_hint_rule: Piece equations NOT labeled — Mathlete reads each piece from graph segments

visual_generators.description: Function graph with two points at x = a and x = b marked; secant line drawn; Mathlete computes [f(b) - f(a)]/(b - a)
visual_generators.format_hint_rule: Slope of secant NOT labeled — Mathlete computes from the two coordinate points

visual_generators.description: Coordinate plane showing a transformed exponential y = a·b^(x-h) + k; horizontal asymptote as dashed line; y-intercept and one other point labeled

svg_params.asymptote: dashed horizontal line at y = k

visual_generators.format_hint_rule: Equation NOT shown — Mathlete identifies transformation parameters from graph features

visual_generators.description: Logarithmic function y = log_b(x-h) + k graphed; vertical asymptote as dashed line; x-intercept labeled
visual_generators.format_hint_rule: Equation NOT shown. VA labeled as 'x = ?' — Mathlete identifies

visual_generators.description: Polynomial function graphed; end behavior shown by arrows; zeros labeled or unlabeled; Mathlete identifies degree/leading coefficient or names end behavior
visual_generators.format_hint_rule: Equation NOT shown. Arrows indicate end behavior — Mathlete writes the description

visual_generators.description: Polynomial graph showing x-intercepts; some cross (odd multiplicity) and some touch/bounce (even multiplicity); Mathlete identifies multiplicity of each zero

svg_params.multiplicity_mix: at least one even, at least one odd

visual_generators.format_hint_rule: Multiplicity numbers NOT shown — Mathlete infers from graph behavior (crossing vs. touching)

visual_generators.description: Rational function graph with vertical asymptotes (dashed), horizontal/slant asymptote (dashed), hole (open circle), and x/y intercepts; Mathlete identifies all features
visual_generators.format_hint_rule: Asymptote equations show 'x = ?' and 'y = ?' — Mathlete writes values from graph

visual_generators.description: Number line or x-axis with critical values marked; sign (+/−) in each interval marked or left blank — Mathlete completes sign chart and writes solution
visual_generators.format_hint_rule: Signs in intervals NOT pre-filled — Mathlete determines and uses to write interval notation solution

visual_generators.description: Full unit circle with all 16 special angles labeled (degrees and radians); one angle highlighted — Mathlete gives exact coordinates or trig value

svg_params.angle_labels: all 16 special angles in degrees AND radians
svg_params.highlighted_angle: one angle marked with arc and label

visual_generators.format_hint_rule: Coordinates NOT shown on unit circle diagram — Mathlete recalls or computes exact values

visual_generators.description: Standard position angle shown in a coordinate plane; arc indicating the angle; Mathlete identifies a coterminal angle
visual_generators.format_hint_rule: Coterminal angle NOT drawn — Mathlete computes ±360° or ±2π and writes the value

visual_generators.description: Sine function graphed on [0, 4π] or [−2π, 2π]; may include vertical/horizontal transformations; Mathlete identifies amplitude, period, phase shift, vertical shift
visual_generators.format_hint_rule: Equation NOT shown — Mathlete writes y = A·sin(Bx + C) + D from visual parameters

visual_generators.description: Cosine function graphed; may be transformed; Mathlete identifies or writes equation
visual_generators.format_hint_rule: Same rules as sine — no equation shown, Mathlete reads parameters from graph

visual_generators.description: Tangent (or cotangent) function graphed over two periods; vertical asymptotes as dashed lines; Mathlete identifies period, asymptotes, and key points
visual_generators.format_hint_rule: Period and asymptote values NOT labeled — Mathlete reads from x-axis positions

visual_generators.description: Labeled non-right triangle with angles A, B, C and opposite sides a, b, c; given values labeled, unknowns as '?'; for SSA shows both possible triangles when applicable

svg_params.ambiguous_case_second_triangle: shown with dashed lines when two solutions

visual_generators.format_hint_rule: Unknown side/angle labeled '?' — Mathlete applies Law of Sines formula. Second triangle in SSA shown ONLY as context, not as answer leak

visual_generators.description: Labeled non-right triangle; SAS shows two sides and included angle; SSS shows all three sides; unknown labeled '?'
visual_generators.format_hint_rule: Formula NOT shown on diagram. Unknown labeled '?'

visual_generators.description: Triangle with two sides and included angle labeled; area formula NOT shown; Mathlete applies Area = (1/2)ab·sin(C)

svg_params.included_angle_C: labeled with arc and degree/radian value

visual_generators.format_hint_rule: Height NOT shown (to prevent A = (1/2)bh approach). Area formula NOT given.

visual_generators.description: Circle graphed on coordinate plane; center and one radius endpoint marked; Mathlete writes standard form equation

svg_params.radius_segment: drawn to edge with length labeled

visual_generators.format_hint_rule: Equation NOT shown — Mathlete writes (x-h)²+(y-k)²=r² from visual

visual_generators.description: Circle with an inscribed angle marked; intercepted arc labeled in degrees; Mathlete computes inscribed angle measure
visual_generators.format_hint_rule: Inscribed angle value NOT shown — Mathlete applies half-arc theorem

visual_generators.description: Circle diagram showing either: chord-chord intersection inside, secant-secant from external point, or tangent-secant; some segments/angles labeled, one as '?'
visual_generators.format_hint_rule: Theorem NOT identified on diagram — Mathlete selects correct relationship from diagram type

visual_generators.description: Circle with a sector shaded; radius labeled; central angle labeled in degrees or radians; Mathlete computes arc length or sector area
visual_generators.format_hint_rule: Arc length and sector area NOT labeled — Mathlete applies correct formula based on units

visual_generators.description: Coordinate plane with triangle vertices labeled A(x₁,y₁), B(x₂,y₂), C(x₃,y₃); Mathlete uses distance formula to classify triangle type
visual_generators.format_hint_rule: Triangle type NOT identified on diagram — Mathlete computes side lengths then classifies

visual_generators.description: Probability distribution table as rendered HTML; x values in first row, P(X=x) in second row; one probability may be missing ('?'); Mathlete computes expected value or finds missing probability
visual_generators.format_hint_rule: E(X) NOT computed on table — Mathlete calculates Σ[x·P(x)]

visual_generators.description: Bell curve with μ and σ marked on x-axis; a shaded region representing a probability area; Mathlete applies Empirical Rule or uses z-table to find the probability
visual_generators.format_hint_rule: Probability percentage NOT shown in shaded region — Mathlete computes

visual_generators.description: Two exponential curves on the same coordinate plane: y = 2^x and y = e^x overlaid; Mathlete identifies which is which and notes that e ≈ 2.718 gives steeper growth than base 2
visual_generators.format_hint_rule: Curve labels NOT shown — Mathlete identifies using relative steepness (e > 2)

visual_generators.description: Side-by-side tables for f and g; one labeled, one unlabeled; Mathlete determines if g is the inverse of f by checking if swapped (x,y) pairs match

svg_params.table_g: same y values now as x, x values as y

visual_generators.format_hint_rule: Relationship between tables NOT annotated — Mathlete checks if (a,b) in f implies (b,a) in g
